description: Commercial office buildings with strong business-hours patterns.

behavioral_rules.name: Business Hours Energy Profile
behavioral_rules.description: 80%+ of daily energy should occur between 08:00-19:00 on weekdays.
behavioral_rules.recommendation: Baseline established — deviations indicate schedule issues.
behavioral_rules.demo_alert: Office follows clean 9-to-5 pattern — {value:.0%} of energy in business hours.

behavioral_rules.name: Monday Morning Ramp-Up
behavioral_rules.description: Monday 07:00-09:00 shows >50% higher load than Tue-Fri equivalent.
behavioral_rules.recommendation: Pre-cool Friday evening to reduce Monday morning HVAC surge.
behavioral_rules.demo_alert: Monday morning surge detected — building systems restarting simultaneously.

behavioral_rules.name: High Night-Time Base Load
behavioral_rules.description: Average consumption between 11PM-5AM exceeds threshold % of daily mean.
behavioral_rules.recommendation: Investigate equipment left running overnight. Schedule HVAC setback.
behavioral_rules.demo_alert: Building is consuming {value}% of daytime energy overnight.

behavioral_rules.name: Weekend Consumption Anomaly
behavioral_rules.description: Weekend usage should be <30% of weekday usage for this building type.
behavioral_rules.recommendation: Review weekend HVAC schedules and unoccupied equipment.
behavioral_rules.demo_alert: Weekend usage is {value}% of weekday — expected <{threshold_pct}%.

efficiency_rules.name: Demand Spike Detection
efficiency_rules.description: Max meter reading exceeds threshold × mean — indicates uncontrolled demand peaks.
efficiency_rules.recommendation: Implement demand response: stagger HVAC start, shift non-critical loads.
efficiency_rules.demo_alert: Peak demand is {value:.1f}x the average — opportunity to flatten load curve.

efficiency_rules.name: Overnight Equipment Load
efficiency_rules.description: After-hours (20:00-06:00) consumption > 15% of daytime suggests equipment not shut down.
efficiency_rules.recommendation: Audit servers, HVAC, and lighting auto-shutoff schedules.
efficiency_rules.demo_alert: Overnight load is {value:.0%} of daytime — check equipment shutdown policy.

weather_sensitivity_rules.name: Weather Sensitivity Check
weather_sensitivity_rules.description: Correlation between air_temperature and meter_reading reveals cooling/heating dependency.
weather_sensitivity_rules.recommendation: Optimize HVAC based on temperature forecast to reduce reactive energy spikes.
weather_sensitivity_rules.demo_alert: Energy use is {value:.0%} correlated with outdoor temperature.

weather_sensitivity_rules.name: Seasonal Energy Pattern
weather_sensitivity_rules.description: Summer (Jun-Aug) vs Winter (Dec-Feb) consumption ratio identifies cooling vs heating dominance.
weather_sensitivity_rules.recommendation: Seasonal HVAC tuning can yield 10-20% energy savings.
weather_sensitivity_rules.demo_alert: Summer usage is {value:.0%} higher than winter — cooling-dominated building.

alert_rules.name: High Consumption During Low-Occupancy Hours
alert_rules.description: Consumption from 21:00-06:00 exceeds 15% of daily average.
alert_rules.recommendation: Configure occupancy sensors and timer-based shutdowns.
alert_rules.demo_alert: Significant energy consumed when building should be empty ({value:.0%} of daily avg).

alert_rules.name: Disproportionate Cooling Load
alert_rules.description: Chilled water usage > 40% of total in mild weather (<22°C).
alert_rules.recommendation: Review thermostat setpoints — natural ventilation viable below 22°C.
alert_rules.demo_alert: Cooling running at full capacity in mild weather — setpoint misconfiguration suspected!
